content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The ___ (happy) on the children's faces when they saw the snow was lovely to see.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot function as the subject noun of a sentence
an adverb cannot fill a noun position as the subject of a clause
incorrect spelling — the correct suffix is -ness, not -yness, and the y changes to i

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Mark works as a ___ (teach) at a primary school in his hometown.
distractor_rationale: a bare verb cannot be used as a noun referring to a person's job title
the gerund form refers to the activity, not to the person who does it
incorrect spelling — the agent suffix for this verb is -er, not -or

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: After years of hard work, the ___ (improve) in her test scores was clear to everyone.
distractor_rationale: a verb cannot function as the subject noun of a sentence
incorrect suffix — the standard noun form of 'improve' uses -ment, not -ness
a present participle cannot serve as the head noun of a noun phrase in this position

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The manager asked the team to ___ (simple) the instructions so that new staff could follow them easily.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb in this structure
a noun cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb required by 'asked the team to ___'
a noun cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb; the verb form with -ify is needed here

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The doctor said that the patient's ___ (able) to walk again would depend on how well the treatment worked.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot serve as the possessive noun head in this noun phrase
incorrect suffix — the standard noun derived from 'able' is 'ability', not 'ableness'
incorrect spelling — the correct derived noun form is 'ability', formed by replacing -le with -ility

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The government's ___ (decide) to build a new hospital was welcomed by local residents.
distractor_rationale: a verb cannot follow a possessive noun phrase as the head of a noun phrase
incorrect suffix — the standard noun form of 'decide' is 'decision', not 'decidement'
an adjective cannot serve as the noun head of a possessive noun phrase in this structure

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Sara hoped to ___ (modern) her kitchen by replacing the old cupboards with new ones.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb required by 'hoped to ___'
a noun cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb in this structure
a noun cannot follow 'to' as the infinitive verb; the -ise verb form is required here

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The ___ (dark) of the sky just before the storm made everyone feel a little nervous.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot function as the subject noun of a sentence with a possessive-of phrase
a verb cannot serve as the subject noun of this sentence; 'darken' is the verb form meaning to become dark
not a real English word — the correct noun suffix for this adjective is -ness, not -ity

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The new software will ___ (digital) all the paper records so they can be stored and searched online.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot follow 'will' as the bare infinitive verb required in this structure
a noun cannot follow 'will' as the bare infinitive verb in this future structure
a noun cannot follow 'will' as the bare infinitive; the -ise verb form is required after a modal

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The ___ (discuss) about working hours lasted over two hours before the team finally reached an agreement.
distractor_rationale: a verb cannot function as the subject noun of a sentence with a prepositional phrase modifier
incorrect suffix — the standard noun form of 'discuss' is 'discussion', not 'discussment'
a present participle cannot serve as the subject noun of a main clause in this structure

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The ___ (happy) on the children's faces when they saw the snow was wonderful to see.
distractor_rationale: an adjective cannot function as the subject noun of the sentence
an adverb cannot occupy a noun position as subject of the clause
this is a common spelling error; the correct suffix is -ness, not -yness

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The team made a big ___ (improve) to their performance after they started training every morning.
distractor_rationale: the base verb cannot follow the article 'a' and act as a noun object
improve does not take the -ness suffix; the correct noun form uses -ment
improve does not take the -ation suffix; this is a false analogy with words like 'information'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The museum hired a new ___ (art) to design the posters for its summer exhibition.
distractor_rationale: the base noun 'art' refers to the subject area, not to a person who practises it; an agent noun is required
the agent suffix for this word is -ist, not -or; 'artor' is not a real English word
the suffix -ment forms abstract nouns from verbs, not agent nouns from other nouns

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The manager asked the team to ___ (simple) the report so that all staff could understand it easily.
distractor_rationale: the adjective 'simple' cannot follow 'to' as an infinitive verb; a verb form is required
the correct verb-forming suffix for this adjective is -ify, not -ize; 'simplize' does not exist
adding -ness forms an abstract noun, not a verb, and a noun cannot follow the infinitive marker 'to' here

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The doctor explained that regular exercise can ___ (strength) the muscles around the knee and reduce pain over time.
distractor_rationale: the noun 'strength' cannot follow a modal verb like 'can' in place of a verb; a verb form is required
'strengthy' is not a real English word; the correct verb is formed with the suffix -en, not -y
the verb-forming suffix for this noun is -en, not -ize; 'strengthize' does not exist in English

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The teacher's ___ of the new students was warm and friendly.
distractor_rationale: welcoming is an adjective/gerund, not a noun suitable for subject position here
welcomed is a past tense verb form, not a noun
welcomement is a non-existent form; learners incorrectly apply -ment to this verb

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the old bridge took almost two years to complete.
distractor_rationale: construct is a base verb and cannot function as a noun subject without an article and noun form
constructing is a gerund/present participle and changes the intended nominal meaning
constructment is a non-existent word; learners wrongly apply -ment instead of -tion

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria showed great ___ when she helped the injured man on the street.
distractor_rationale: kind is an adjective and cannot fill a noun object position after 'great'
kindly is an adverb and cannot follow 'great' as a noun
kindity is a non-existent form; learners incorrectly apply -ity to this adjective instead of -ness

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The science ___ explained the results of his experiment to a group of students at the university.
distractor_rationale: research is a noun/verb base form and does not refer to a person who performs the action
researching is a present participle and cannot function as an agent noun
researchist is a non-existent word; learners incorrectly apply -ist instead of -er for this agent noun

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The local government made a ___ to improve public transport in the city centre.
distractor_rationale: decide is a base verb and cannot function as the object noun of 'made'
decisive is an adjective and does not fit the noun object slot after 'made a'
decidement is a non-existent form; learners wrongly apply -ment rather than -sion to this verb

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After months of hard work, the team finally saw a clear ___ in the quality of their products.
distractor_rationale: improve is a base verb and cannot function as a noun after 'a clear'
improveness is a non-existent word; learners wrongly apply -ness instead of -ment to this verb
improving is a gerund/present participle and does not collocate naturally with 'a clear' in this context

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The government needs to ___ its energy policy if it wants to reduce pollution over the next decade.
distractor_rationale: modern is an adjective and cannot fill a verb slot after 'needs to'
modernity is a noun and cannot function as a verb in this infinitive position
modernization is a noun and cannot follow 'needs to' as a verb infinitive

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the new hospital director brought significant changes to how staff were managed.
distractor_rationale: appoint is a base verb and cannot serve as the subject noun of the sentence
appointness is a non-existent form; learners incorrectly apply -ness instead of -ment to this verb
appointing is a gerund that would require a possessive determiner and changes the register awkwardly in this formal context

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The piano ___ at the concert hall has been teaching young musicians for over thirty years.
distractor_rationale: instruct is a base verb and cannot refer to a person in subject position
instruction is a noun meaning the act or content of teaching, not the person who teaches
instructist is a non-existent word; learners incorrectly apply -ist instead of -or for this agent noun

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The new manager tried to ___ the filing system so that all staff could find documents more easily.
distractor_rationale: simple is an adjective and cannot fill the verb slot after 'tried to'
simpleness is a noun and cannot function as a verb infinitive after 'tried to'
simplification is a noun and cannot follow 'tried to' as a verb infinitive
